headline: Tougher public safeguards urged for digital health data
summary: Lancet report argues that governments should limit data extraction of powerful tech companies
body_text:
Countries must strengthen public safeguards on the use of digital health data to avoid growing inequalities in medical outcomes and potential human rights abuses, a group of leading independent experts has warned.

Governments should enhance transparency and accountability in the use of artificial intelligence in healthcare, create new institutions to oversee health information collected by tech companies and foster “data solidarity” to balance commercial with public benefits, they said.

The recommendations were produced by the Lancet and Financial Times Commission on Governing Health Futures, which convened 19 specialists from 14 countries to review recent trends. They consulted widely, including with youth groups from around the world.
